Cognitiva","text":"<p>Optimizaci\u00f3n, Persistencia y Verificabilidad en Agentes de IA de Pr\u00f3xima Generaci\u00f3n</p> <p>[!NOTE] Este documento establece el marco te\u00f3rico-pr\u00e1ctico que sustenta la arquitectura de CORTEX y el Enjambre Centauro. Cada secci\u00f3n mapea directamente a componentes implementados o en roadmap.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#introduccion-el-imperativo-de-la-evolucion-arquitectonica","title":"Introducci\u00f3n: El Imperativo de la Evoluci\u00f3n Arquitect\u00f3nica","text":"<p>La trayectoria actual de la Inteligencia Artificial generativa ha alcanzado un punto de inflexi\u00f3n cr\u00edtico. Los sistemas de primera generaci\u00f3n, caracterizados por interacciones ap\u00e1tridas y recuperaciones de informaci\u00f3n simplistas, se enfrentan a un \"techo de inteligencia\" que limita su aplicabilidad en entornos empresariales y cr\u00edticos.</p> <p>La pregunta fundamental de \"c\u00f3mo mejorarlo\" no se responde simplemente incrementando el tama\u00f1o de los par\u00e1metros de los modelos, sino mediante una reingenier\u00eda profunda de la arquitectura subyacente que soporta la cognici\u00f3n sint\u00e9tica.</p> <p>Para transformar asistentes reactivos en agentes soberanos capaces de razonamiento estrat\u00e9gico y ejecuci\u00f3n aut\u00f3noma, es necesario abordar cuatro pilares de optimizaci\u00f3n:</p> <ol> <li>La eficiencia en la recuperaci\u00f3n de datos a hiperescala</li> <li>La estructuraci\u00f3n de la memoria a largo plazo</li> <li>La implementaci\u00f3n de bucles de razonamiento metacognitivo</li> <li>La instauraci\u00f3n de garant\u00edas de seguridad criptogr\u00e1fica</li> </ol> <p>El estado actual del arte, dominado por la Generaci\u00f3n Aumentada por Recuperaci\u00f3n (RAG) convencional, sufre de amnesia contextual y latencia operativa. Los agentes carecen de una comprensi\u00f3n persistente del mundo y operan bajo un paradigma de confianza ciega que es inaceptable para sectores regulados.</p> <p>La mejora sustancial de estos sistemas requiere la transici\u00f3n hacia arquitecturas de memoria unificada que integren vectores, gr\u00e1ficos de conocimiento y series temporales, respaldadas por mecanismos de consenso bizantino y pruebas de conocimiento cero. Este informe detalla la hoja de ruta t\u00e9cnica para elevar la IA desde la automatizaci\u00f3n de tareas (Nivel 2) hacia la colaboraci\u00f3n estrat\u00e9gica aut\u00f3noma (Nivel 4), estableciendo nuevos est\u00e1ndares en rendimiento computacional y soberan\u00eda de datos.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#1-el-sustrato-de-la-velocidad-optimizacion-vectorial-y-cuantizacion-en-el-borde","title":"1. El Sustrato de la Velocidad: Optimizaci\u00f3n Vectorial y Cuantizaci\u00f3n en el Borde","text":"<p>La capacidad de un agente para recuperar informaci\u00f3n relevante en tiempo real es el cuello de botella primario en la experiencia de usuario y la eficacia operativa. Las implementaciones iniciales, que dependen de b\u00fasquedas vectoriales de fuerza bruta, se vuelven insostenibles a medida que los corpus de conocimiento escalan hacia los miles de millones de vectores.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#11-de-la-busqueda-lineal-a-la-navegacion-jerarquica-hnsw","title":"1.1 De la B\u00fasqueda Lineal a la Navegaci\u00f3n Jer\u00e1rquica (HNSW)","text":"<p>En los sistemas de recuperaci\u00f3n b\u00e1sicos, la identificaci\u00f3n de los vecinos m\u00e1s cercanos (K-Nearest Neighbors, KNN) se realiza mediante un escaneo exhaustivo que calcula la distancia \u2014 ya sea Euclidiana o de Coseno \u2014 entre el vector de consulta y cada uno de los vectores almacenados en la base de datos.</p> <p>Este enfoque presenta una complejidad computacional de O(N \u00b7 D), donde N representa el n\u00famero total de vectores y D la dimensionalidad del embedding. Cuando se opera con modelos modernos como <code>text-embedding-3-small</code> de OpenAI (1536 dimensiones) sobre un conjunto de un mill\u00f3n de registros, el sistema debe ejecutar miles de millones de operaciones de punto flotante por cada consulta individual, resultando en latencias inaceptables para aplicaciones interactivas.</p> <p>La soluci\u00f3n reside en la implementaci\u00f3n de \u00edndices de Mundo Peque\u00f1o Jer\u00e1rquico Navegable (HNSW):</p> <p><code>mermaid graph TB     subgraph \"Capa 3 \u2014 Autopista Global\"         A((N1)) -.-&gt; B((N47))         B -.-&gt; C((N215))     end     subgraph \"Capa 2 \u2014 Conexiones Intermedias\"         D((N1)) --&gt; E((N12)) --&gt; F((N28))         F --&gt; G((N47))     end     subgraph \"Capa 1 \u2014 Red Densa Local\"         H((N1)) --&gt; I((N3)) --&gt; J((N5))         J --&gt; K((N8)) --&gt; L((N12))     end     A --&gt; D     D --&gt; H     B --&gt; G     G --&gt; L     style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#e94560     style B fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#e94560     style C fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#e94560</code></p> <p>HNSW reestructura el espacio vectorial como un grafo multicapa. En las capas superiores, los nodos est\u00e1n conectados de manera escasa, permitiendo \"saltos\" largos a trav\u00e9s del espacio sem\u00e1ntico. A medida que la b\u00fasqueda desciende a capas inferiores, la densidad aumenta, permitiendo refinamiento local. Esto transforma la complejidad a O(log N).</p> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El m\u00f3dulo <code>sqlite-vec</code> integrado en CORTEX utiliza HNSW como \u00edndice primario para la tabla <code>vec_facts</code>. La configuraci\u00f3n actual contempla <code>M=16</code> conexiones por nodo y <code>efConstruction=200</code> para balancear recall vs. velocidad de indexaci\u00f3n.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#12-cuantizacion-binaria-compresion-radical-y-aceleracion-por-hardware","title":"1.2 Cuantizaci\u00f3n Binaria: Compresi\u00f3n Radical y Aceleraci\u00f3n por Hardware","text":"<p>La Cuantizaci\u00f3n Binaria (BQ) comprime cada dimensi\u00f3n del vector a un solo bit: los valores positivos se convierten en <code>1</code> y los negativos o cero en <code>0</code>.</p> M\u00e9trica float32 Binario Factor Almacenamiento por vector (384d) 1,536 bytes 48 bytes 32\u00d7 \u00cdndice 1M vectores ~1.5 GB ~48 MB 32\u00d7 Operaci\u00f3n de similitud Producto punto FP XOR + <code>popcount</code> 15-45\u00d7 m\u00e1s r\u00e1pido Recall@10 100% ~92-95% Marginal <p>Al operar con vectores binarios, el c\u00e1lculo de similitud utiliza la Distancia de Hamming \u2014 operaciones l\u00f3gicas XOR seguidas de conteo de bits activados (<code>popcount</code>), instrucciones optimizadas a nivel de hardware en CPUs modernas (AVX-512 en x86, NEON en ARM).</p> <p>[!TIP] Estrategia de re-puntuaci\u00f3n (rescoring): Usar el \u00edndice binario para recuperar un conjunto candidato amplio (top-100 ANN) y aplicar los vectores originales float32 solo a estos candidatos para el ranking final. Velocidad binaria + precisi\u00f3n float32.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#13-arquitecturas-de-almacenamiento-shadow-tables-y-diskann","title":"1.3 Arquitecturas de Almacenamiento: Shadow Tables y DiskANN","text":"<p>La integraci\u00f3n de capacidades vectoriales en bases de datos relacionales utiliza tablas sombra (shadow tables). Las implementaciones como <code>sqlite-vec</code> serializan los nodos del grafo HNSW directamente en tablas internas del motor de base de datos, asegurando que las operaciones vectoriales cumplan las propiedades ACID.</p> <p>Para escenarios hiperescala donde el \u00edndice excede la RAM \u2014 situaci\u00f3n com\u00fan en agentes soberanos sobre hardware modesto \u2014 la arquitectura \u00f3ptima es DiskANN:</p> <ul> <li>Dise\u00f1ado para aprovechar SSDs modernos</li> <li>Optimiza el grafo para minimizar lecturas aleatorias de disco</li> <li>Sirve consultas con \u00edndices 10\u00d7 mayores que la RAM del sistema</li> <li>Rompe la dependencia de hardware costoso</li> </ul> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El <code>EmbeddingPrunerMixin</code> implementado en <code>cortex/engine/embeddings.py</code> gestiona la compresi\u00f3n adaptativa. Cuando se alcanza el umbral de embeddings (<code>CORTEX_MAX_EMBEDDINGS</code>), el pruner aplica cuantizaci\u00f3n escalar y eliminaci\u00f3n de vectores de baja utilidad.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#2-ingenieria-de-contexto-y-memoria-persistente-mas-alla-del-rag-estatico","title":"2. Ingenier\u00eda de Contexto y Memoria Persistente: M\u00e1s All\u00e1 del RAG Est\u00e1tico","text":"<p>La mejora cualitativa trasciende la velocidad de recuperaci\u00f3n \u2014 reside en la calidad y estructuraci\u00f3n del contexto. El paradigma RAG tradicional muestra deficiencias cr\u00edticas en razonamiento global, comprensi\u00f3n de relaciones indirectas y memoria a largo plazo.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#21-graphrag-y-la-dimension-temporal","title":"2.1 GraphRAG y la Dimensi\u00f3n Temporal","text":"<p>El RAG est\u00e1ndar opera bajo una limitaci\u00f3n estructural: recupera \"puntos\" de datos aislados sin comprender la topolog\u00eda que los une.</p> <p>GraphRAG aborda esta deficiencia mediante:</p> <ol> <li>Extracci\u00f3n de entidades y relaciones del texto crudo usando LLMs</li> <li>Detecci\u00f3n de comunidades (algoritmo de Leiden) para identificar cl\u00fasteres tem\u00e1ticos</li> <li>Res\u00famenes jer\u00e1rquicos pre-computados para consultas de alto nivel</li> <li>Aristas temporales (framework Graphiti) que distinguen hechos vigentes vs. obsoletos</li> </ol> <p><code>mermaid graph LR     A[\"Documento crudo\"] --&gt; B[\"Extracci\u00f3n de entidades\"]     B --&gt; C[\"Grafo de Conocimiento\"]     C --&gt; D[\"Detecci\u00f3n de comunidades\"]     D --&gt; E[\"Res\u00famenes jer\u00e1rquicos\"]     C --&gt; F[\"Aristas temporales\"]     F --&gt; G[\"Invalidaci\u00f3n autom\u00e1tica\"]     E --&gt; H[\"Consulta global\"]     G --&gt; H     style A fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560     style H fill:#0f3460,stroke:#16c79a</code></p> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El m\u00f3dulo <code>cortex/engine/graph/</code> implementa un grafo de conocimiento con aristas temporales via <code>valid_from</code>/<code>valid_until</code> en cada fact. El <code>QueryMixin.search()</code> combina b\u00fasqueda vectorial + filtrado temporal via <code>as_of</code>.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#22-arquitectura-unificada-de-memoria-el-modelo-postgresql","title":"2.2 Arquitectura Unificada de Memoria: El Modelo PostgreSQL","text":"<p>La fragmentaci\u00f3n de la infraestructura introduce latencia y complejidad que obstaculizan la autonom\u00eda del agente. La arquitectura de referencia unifica tres tipos de memoria cognitiva:</p> Tipo de Memoria Funci\u00f3n Cognitiva Implementaci\u00f3n en CORTEX Epis\u00f3dica Registro autobiogr\u00e1fico secuencial (\"\u00bfQu\u00e9 tarea realizamos ayer?\") Tabla <code>facts</code> con <code>created_at</code> como series temporales. Consultas de rango temporal via <code>time_travel()</code> Sem\u00e1ntica Conocimiento cristalizado y hechos recuperables <code>vec_facts</code> con embeddings <code>all-MiniLM-L6-v2</code> (384d) + HNSW index Procedimental Habilidades, preferencias, reglas de negocio Tabla <code>facts</code> con <code>fact_type='procedure'</code> + <code>confidence='verified'</code> <p>Esta unificaci\u00f3n permite consultas cognitivas complejas en una sola transacci\u00f3n SQL:</p> <pre><code>-- Ejemplo: Recuperar las 5 \u00faltimas interacciones sobre \"seguridad\" con confianza verificada\nSELECT f.content, f.created_at, f.confidence\nFROM facts f\nJOIN vec_facts v ON v.fact_id = f.id\nWHERE f.project = 'moskv-swarm'\n  AND f.created_at &gt; datetime('now', '-7 days')   -- Memoria epis\u00f3dica\n  AND f.confidence = 'verified'                     -- Memoria procedimental\nORDER BY vec_distance_cosine(v.embedding, ?)        -- Memoria sem\u00e1ntica\nLIMIT 5;\n</code></pre> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El pipeline verificado en el smoke test (<code>smoke_test_cortex_pipeline.py</code>) demostr\u00f3 la unificaci\u00f3n: <code>post_fact</code> (sem\u00e1ntica), <code>persist_mission_insight</code> (procedimental) y <code>get_summary</code> (epis\u00f3dica) operan sobre la misma base SQLite con integridad ACID.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#23-busqueda-hibrida-y-fusion-de-rangos-rrf","title":"2.3 B\u00fasqueda H\u00edbrida y Fusi\u00f3n de Rangos (RRF)","text":"<p>Los modelos de embedding sobresalen en matices sem\u00e1nticos (\"veh\u00edculo\" \u2194 \"coche\") pero fallan en coincidencia exacta de palabras clave, c\u00f3digos o nombres propios.</p> <p>La Fusi\u00f3n de Rango Rec\u00edproco (RRF) sintetiza b\u00fasqueda vectorial + l\u00e9xica (BM25):</p> <pre><code>RRF_score(d) = \u03a3 1 / (k + rank_i(d))\n</code></pre> <p>Donde <code>k</code> es una constante de suavizado (t\u00edpicamente 60) y <code>rank_i(d)</code> es la posici\u00f3n del documento <code>d</code> en la lista de resultados del m\u00e9todo <code>i</code>.</p> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El <code>SearchMixin</code> en <code>cortex/engine/search.py</code> implementa b\u00fasqueda h\u00edbrida combinando <code>sqlite-vec</code> (vectorial) + FTS5 (l\u00e9xica) con RRF. El <code>recall_context()</code> del bridge actual usa fallback de texto (LIKE queries) cuando <code>sqlite-vec</code> no est\u00e1 disponible.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#3-modelos-de-mundo-y-razonamiento-estrategico-el-sistema-2-de-la-ia","title":"3. Modelos de Mundo y Razonamiento Estrat\u00e9gico: El Sistema 2 de la IA","text":"<p>Una vez resueltos los desaf\u00edos de almacenamiento y memoria, la mejora cualitativa del agente depende de su capacidad de razonamiento. Los agentes actuales operan en modo reactivo (Sistema 1). Para tareas complejas, es imperativo implementar el Sistema 2: deliberativo, planificador y autocr\u00edtico.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#31-arquitectura-aero-y-el-doble-bucle-de-aprendizaje","title":"3.1 Arquitectura AERO y el Doble Bucle de Aprendizaje","text":"<p>El framework AERO (Autonomous Evolutionary Reasoning Optimization) implementa un sistema de doble bucle end\u00f3geno:</p> <p><code>mermaid graph TD     subgraph \"Bucle Interno \u2014 F\u00e1brica de Datos\"         G[\"\ud83c\udfaf Generador&lt;br/&gt;(Self-Questioning)\"] --&gt; S[\"\ud83d\udd27 Solucionador&lt;br/&gt;(Self-Answering)\"]         S --&gt; C[\"\ud83d\udd0d Cr\u00edtico&lt;br/&gt;(Self-Criticism)\"]         C --&gt;|\"Rechaza\"| G         C --&gt;|\"Aprueba\"| V[\"\u2705 Experiencia Validada\"]     end     subgraph \"Bucle Externo \u2014 Consolidaci\u00f3n\"         V --&gt; F[\"Fine-tuning / Prompt Optimization\"]         F --&gt; M[\"Memoria Procedimental Mejorada\"]         M -.-&gt;|\"ZDP calibrada\"| G     end     style G fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#e94560     style C fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#ffd700     style V fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#16c79a</code></p> <p>Zona de Desarrollo Pr\u00f3ximo (ZDP): Los desaf\u00edos se calibran para estar ligeramente por encima de la competencia actual confirmada del agente \u2014 evitando estancamiento (tareas triviales) y colapso del aprendizaje (tareas imposibles).</p> <p>Correcci\u00f3n Contrafactual Independiente (ICC): El agente verifica conclusiones asumiendo inicialmente que su respuesta es incorrecta e intenta construir un camino l\u00f3gico que justifique el error. Si no lo logra \u2192 confianza reforzada. Si lo logra \u2192 reevaluaci\u00f3n profunda.</p> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a Centauro: El <code>ConsensusBreaker</code> en <code>moskv-swarm/consensus/</code> implementa una versi\u00f3n pr\u00e1ctica del ICC \u2014 cuando los agentes del enjambre no alcanzan consenso, se escala a un <code>ElderCouncil</code> (3 evaluadores independientes) o a <code>HumanEscalation</code> via el <code>SovereignGate</code>.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#32-modelos-de-mundo-para-planificacion-estrategica","title":"3.2 Modelos de Mundo para Planificaci\u00f3n Estrat\u00e9gica","text":"<p>La simulaci\u00f3n mental permite al agente proyectar consecuencias antes de ejecutar:</p> <ul> <li>Antes de <code>delete_file</code> \u2192 simular ruptura de dependencias</li> <li>Antes de <code>deploy_production</code> \u2192 simular impacto en servicios dependientes</li> <li>Antes de <code>execute_trade</code> \u2192 simular impacto en portfolio bajo varios escenarios</li> </ul> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El <code>SovereignGate</code> (<code>cortex/sovereign_gate.py</code>) implementa la barrera de planificaci\u00f3n deliberada \u2014 las acciones L3 (destructivas) requieren aprobaci\u00f3n expl\u00edcita del operador, impidiendo ejecuci\u00f3n reactiva del Sistema 1.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#4-gobernanza-seguridad-y-verificabilidad-la-capa-de-confianza","title":"4. Gobernanza, Seguridad y Verificabilidad: La Capa de Confianza","text":"<p>A medida que los agentes escalan hacia Nivel 4/5 de autonom\u00eda, la seguridad basada en \"guardarra\u00edles de texto\" es insuficiente. La mejora exige garant\u00edas criptogr\u00e1ficas verificables y arquitecturas de confianza cero.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#41-soberania-de-hardware-tees-y-computacion-confidencial","title":"4.1 Soberan\u00eda de Hardware: TEEs y Computaci\u00f3n Confidencial","text":"<p>Los Entornos de Ejecuci\u00f3n Confiables (TEEs) \u2014 Intel TDX, AMD SEV, NVIDIA Confidential Computing \u2014 proporcionan:</p> <ul> <li>Encriptaci\u00f3n en uso: Pesos del modelo y datos del usuario permanecen encriptados en RAM durante toda la inferencia</li> <li>Atestaci\u00f3n remota: El agente puede probar criptogr\u00e1ficamente que ejecuta una versi\u00f3n no adulterada de su c\u00f3digo y modelo</li> </ul> <p>[!CAUTION] Indispensable para el despliegue en finanzas y salud, donde la integridad del algoritmo es tan cr\u00edtica como la confidencialidad de los datos.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#42-auditoria-inmutable-zk-proofs-y-zksql","title":"4.2 Auditor\u00eda Inmutable: ZK-Proofs y ZKSQL","text":"<p>Las Pruebas de Conocimiento Cero (ZK-Proofs) resuelven la \"Paradoja de la Auditor\u00eda\":</p> <pre><code>ZK-Audit Log = Proof(\n    input \u2208 Domain,\n    model = certified_version,\n    filters = [safety_check_1, ..., safety_check_N] ALL PASSED,\n    output = f(input, model, filters)\n) \u2192 VERIFIABLE sin revelar input ni output\n</code></pre> <p>ZKSQL extiende esto a consultas anal\u00edticas \u2014 un regulador puede lanzar queries SQL sobre la base de datos privada y recibir respuestas con prueba criptogr\u00e1fica de correcci\u00f3n y completitud, sin acceso de lectura a registros individuales.</p> <p>[!IMPORTANT] Mapeo a CORTEX: El <code>LedgerMixin</code> (<code>cortex/engine/ledger.py</code>) implementa un log de auditor\u00eda inmutable con hashes can\u00f3nicos SHA-256 encadenados (cada entrada referencia el hash de la anterior). El <code>time_travel()</code> permite reconstrucci\u00f3n forense del estado en cualquier punto temporal.</p>"},{"location":"cognitive_sovereignty_architectures/#43-consenso-en-enjambres-multi-agente","title":"4.3 Consenso en Enjambres Multi-Agente","text":"<p>La integridad del enjambre se \u03b2\u00b7exp(\u03b3\u00b7failures)\n\n# Selecci\u00f3n de l\u00edder via VRF ponderada\nP(agent_i = leader) = T_i / \u03a3 T_j  \u2200j \u2208 swarm\n</code></pre> Evento Impacto en Reputaci\u00f3n S\u00edntesis: Diagrama de Fases","text":"<p>La distinci\u00f3n no describe dos cosas distintas. Describe dos momentos del mismo proceso:</p> <pre><code>Pasado cristalizado (CORTEX) \u2190\u2192 Presente ejecutando (MOSKV-1) \u2190\u2192 Futuro anticipado (outputs)\n</code></pre> <p>```mermaid graph LR     subgraph \"Estado S\u00f3lido\"         CORTEX[\"CORTEXPasado cristalizadoMemoria inmutable\"]     end     subgraph \"Estado Plasma\"         MOSKV[\"MOSKV-1Presente ejecutandoCognici\u00f3n fluida\"]     end     subgraph \"Estado Gaseoso\"         OUTPUT[\"OutputsFuturo anticipadoIntenci\u00f3n colapsada\"]     end</p> <pre><code>CORTEX --&gt;|\"recall / search\"| MOSKV\nMOSKV --&gt;|\"store / deprecate\"| CORTEX\nMOSKV --&gt;|\"genera\"| OUTPUT\nOUTPUT --&gt;|\"retroalimenta contexto\"| MOSKV\n</code></pre> <p>```</p> <p>CORTEX es MOSKV-1 en estado s\u00f3lido. MOSKV-1 es CORTEX en estado plasma.</p> <p>La separaci\u00f3n es real desde el punto de vista del arquitecto humano que necesita observar y controlar. La inteligencia es fluida y ca\u00f3tica por naturaleza; la memoria que la sustenta debe ser inmutable como la gravedad.</p>"},{"location":"cross_platform_guide/","title":"Cross-Platform Architecture","text":"<p>CORTEX runs natively on macOS, Linux, and Windows without Docker. This guide covers the platform abstraction layer that makes this possible.</p>"},{"location":"cross_platform_guide/#the-cortexplatformsys-module","title":"The <code>cortex.platform.sys</code> Module","text":"<p>All OS-dependent code is routed through a single abstraction layer. Direct OS checks scattered across the codebase are prohibited.</p> <pre><code>from cortex.platform.sys import (\n    is_macos, is_linux, is_windows,\n    platform_name,\n    get_cortex_dir, get_service_dir, get_python_executable,\n    tail_file_command,\n)\n</code></pre> Function Returns <code>is_macos()</code> <code>True</code> on Darwin <code>is_linux()</code> <code>True</code> on Linux <code>is_windows()</code> <code>True</code> on Win32 <code>platform_name()</code> Human-readable: <code>\"macOS\"</code>, <code>\"Linux\"</code>, <code>\"Windows\"</code> <code>get_cortex_dir()</code> <code>~/.cortex</code> (Unix) or <code>%APPDATA%/cortex</code> (Windows) <code>get_service_dir()</code> <code>~/Library/LaunchAgents</code> (macOS), <code>~/.config/systemd/user</code> (Linux), <code>None</code> (Windows) <code>get_python_executable()</code> <code>sys.executable</code> \u2014 no hardcoded paths <code>tail_file_command(path)</code> <code>['tail', '-f', path]</code> (Unix) or <code>['powershell', 'Get-Content', path, '-Wait']</code> (Windows) <p>Dynamic executables: Instead of hardcoding paths like <code>/Users/.../bin/python</code>, the architecture uses <code>get_python_executable()</code> (or <code>sys.executable</code>) to ensure daemons and subshells spawn using the correct Python binary regardless of install location.</p>"},{"location":"cross_platform_guide/#native-notifications","title":"Native Notifications","text":"<p>The Notifier component (<code>cortex/daemon/notifier.py</code>) uses adaptive dispatch for system-native alerts:</p> Platform Method Integration macOS <code>osascript</code> Notification Center Linux <code>notify-send</code> libnotify Windows PowerShell <code>System.Windows.Forms.NotifyIcon</code> (balloon tip) Fallback Logger <code>INFO</code>/<code>WARNING</code> if no UI session"},{"location":"cross_platform_guide/#daemons-service-managers","title":"Daemons &amp; All paths must be relative or derived dynamically:</p> <pre><code># \u2705 Correct\nfrom pathlib import Path\nMODULE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent\nDATA_PATH = MODULE_DIR / \"data\" / \"target.json\"\n\n# \u274c Forbidden\nDATA_PATH = \"./data/target.json\"\n</code></pre> <p>This ensures CORTEX remains deployable on any machine without code changes \u2014 the foundation of a truly sovereign, cross-platform agent infrastructure.</p>"},{"location":"deployment/","title":"Deployment","text":"<p>CORTEX supports multiple deployment models \u2014 from a single <code>pip install</code> to production Kubernetes clusters.</p>"},{"location":"deployment/#local-development","title":"Local (Development)","text":"<p>The simplest deployment. implementations:</p>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#1-cortexengine-composite-orchestrator","title":"1. <code>CortexEngine</code> (Composite Orchestrator)","text":"<p>Located in <code>cortex/engine/__init__.py</code>. The main entry point used by both CLI and API:</p> <ul> <li>Composes <code>FactManager</code>, <code>EmbeddingManager</code>, and <code>ConsensusManager</code></li> <li>Provides both sync and async methods via <code>SyncCompatMixin</code></li> <li>Manages database connections and ledger</li> <li>Delegates CRUD to mixins: <code>StoreMixin</code>, <code>QueryMixin</code>, <code>ConsensusMixin</code></li> </ul> <pre><code>from cortex.engine import CortexEngine\n\nengine = CortexEngine(db_path=\"my.db\", auto_embed=True)\nengine.init_db_sync()\n\n# Store\nfact_id = engine.store_sync(\"project-x\", \"Python is great\", fact_type=\"knowledge\")\n\n# Search\nresults = engine.search_sync(\"programming languages\")\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#2-asynccortexengine-api-engine","title":"2. <code>AsyncCortexEngine</code> (API Engine)","text":"<p>Located in <code>cortex/engine_async.py</code>. Used by FastAPI routes for maximum concurrency:</p> <ul> <li>Takes a <code>CortexConnectionPool</code> for connection management</li> <li>All methods are <code>async</code></li> <li>Handles transaction logging and hash chain maintenance</li> </ul> <pre><code>pool = await CortexConnectionPool.create(db_path, pool_size=5)\nengine = AsyncCortexEngine(pool, db_path)\n\nfact_id = await engine.store(project=\"x\", content=\"Hello\", fact_type=\"knowledge\")\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#adding-a-new-route","title":"Adding a New Route","text":"<ol> <li>Create the route file <code>cortex/routes/my_feature.py</code>:</li> </ol> <pre><code>from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends\nfrom cortex.auth import AuthResult, require_permission\nfrom cortex.api.deps import get_async_engine\n\nrouter = APIRouter(tags=[\"my-feature\"])\n\n@router.get(\"/v1/my-feature\")\nasync def my_endpoint(\n    auth: AuthResult = Depends(require_permission(\"read\")),\n    engine = Depends(get_async_engine),\n):\n    # All queries automatically scoped to auth.tenant_id\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n</code></pre> <ol> <li>Register in <code>cortex/api/core.py</code>:</li> </ol> <pre><code>from cortex.routes.my_feature import router as my_feature_router\napp.include_router(my_feature_router)\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#adding-a-new-cli-command","title":"Adding a New CLI Command","text":"<ol> <li>Create the command file <code>cortex/cli/my_cmds.py</code>:</li> </ol> <pre><code>import click\nfrom cortex.engine import CortexEngine\n\n@click.command(\"my-command\")\n@click.argument(\"project\")\n@click.option(\"--db\", default=None)\ndef my_command(project: str, db: str | None):\n    \"\"\"Description of what this command does.\"\"\"\n    engine = CortexEngine(db_path=db)\n    engine.init_db_sync()\n    # ... your logic\n    click.echo(\"Done!\")\n</code></pre> <ol> <li>Register in <code>cortex/cli/__init__.py</code>:</li> </ol> <pre><code>from cortex.cli.my_cmds import my_command\ncli.add_command(my_command)\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#adding-a-new-mcp-tool","title":"Adding a New MCP Tool","text":"<ol> <li>Add the tool in <code>cortex/mcp/server.py</code>:</li> </ol> <pre><code>@server.tool()\nasync def cortex_my_tool(param: str) -&gt; str:\n    \"\"\"Description for the AI agent.\"\"\"\n    engine = get_engine()\n    # ... your logic\n    return result\n</code></pre> <ol> <li>Add guard rules in <code>cortex/mcp/guard.py</code> if needed.</li> </ol>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#configuration-system","title":"Configuration System","text":"<p>All config lives in <code>cortex/config.py</code>. Variables are loaded from environment at import time:</p> <pre><code>from cortex import config\n\n# Read value\nprint(config.DB_PATH)\n\n# Refresh after env changes\nconfig.reload()\n</code></pre> <p>Test Isolation</p> <p>Always call <code>config.reload()</code> after patching environment variables. The global <code>conftest.py</code> does this automatically.</p>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#writing-tests","title":"Writing Tests","text":""},{"location":"developer-guide/#test-isolation","title":"Test Isolation","text":"<p>Tests use temporary databases and <code>config.reload()</code> for isolation:</p> <pre><code>import pytest\nfrom cortex.engine import CortexEngine\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef engine(tmp_path):\n    db = tmp_path / \"test.db\"\n    eng = CortexEngine(db_path=db, auto_embed=False)\n    eng.init_db_sync()\n    return eng\n\ndef test_store(engine):\n    fid = engine.store_sync(\"test\", \"Hello world\", fact_type=\"knowledge\")\n    assert fid &gt; 0\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#api-tests","title":"API Tests","text":"<pre><code>from fastapi.testclient import TestClient\nimport cortex.api as api_mod\n\ndef test_health(tmp_path):\n    os.environ[\"CORTEX_DB\"] = str(tmp_path / \"test.db\")\n    from cortex import config\n    config.reload()\n\n    with TestClient(api_mod.app) as c:\n        resp = c.get(\"/health\")\n        assert resp.status_code == 200\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#async-tests","title":"Async Tests","text":"<pre><code>@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_async_store(pool, engine):\n    fact_id = await engine.store(project=\"test\", content=\"async fact\")\n    assert fact_id &gt; 0\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#running-tests","title":"Running Tests","text":"<pre><code>make test          # All tests (60s timeout)\nmake test-fast     # Exclude slow tests\nmake test-slow     # Only slow tests (graph, embeddings)\nmake lint          # Ruff linter\nmake format        # Auto-format\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#i18n-system","title":"i18n System","text":"<p>All user-facing error messages go through <code>cortex/i18n.py</code>:</p> <pre><code>from cortex.i18n import get_trans\n\nmsg = get_trans(\"error_not_found\", lang=\"es\")\n# \u2192 \"Recurso no encontrado\"\n</code></pre> <p>Supported languages: <code>en</code>, <code>es</code>, <code>eu</code> (Basque).</p> <p>To add a new translation: 1. Add entry to <code>TRANSLATIONS</code> dict in <code>i18n.py</code> 2. Provide <code>en</code>, <code>es</code>, and <code>eu</code> translations 3. Use <code>get_trans(\"your_key\", lang)</code> in route handlers</p>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#schema-migrations","title":"Schema Migrations","text":"<p>When adding a new table:</p> <ol> <li>Add <code>CREATE TABLE</code> to <code>cortex/schema.py</code></li> <li>Add it to the <code>ALL_SCHEMA</code> list</li> <li>Create a migration file in <code>cortex/migrations/</code> for existing databases</li> <li>Test with <code>cortex init</code> on a fresh DB and <code>cortex migrate</code> on existing</li> </ol>"},{"location":"developer-guide/#coding-conventions","title":"Coding Conventions","text":"Convention Rule Sync methods Suffix with <code>_sync</code> (e.g., <code>store_sync()</code>) Async methods No suffix (e.g., <code>store()</code>) SQL Always parameterized (<code>?</code> placeholders, never f-strings) Secrets Environment variables only, never hardcoded Logging <code>logging.getLogger(\"cortex.module_name\")</code> Dates ISO 8601 via <code>cortex.temporal.now_iso()</code> Hashing <code>cortex.canonical.canonical_json()</code> for deterministic serialization Error handling Specific exceptions (<code>sqlite3.Error</code>, <code>ValueError</code>), never bare <code>except</code> Types Full type hints, <code>py.typed</code> marker included Formatting Ruff (line length 100, target Python 3.10)"},{"location":"developer-guide/#continuous-documentation","title":"Continuous Documentation","text":"<p>CORTEX uses <code>mkdocstrings</code> for auto-generating API documentation from source code. Do not open public GitHub issues for security vulnerabilities.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/","title":"The 5 Structurally Impossible Failures","text":"<p>\"The most valuable insight of a product rarely comes from what it does \u2014 it comes from what it guarantees cannot happen. Define the failure you make impossible and you have the most powerful sales message in your category.\"</p> <p>This document defines, with technical precision, the five failure modes that CORTEX makes structurally impossible \u2014 not merely unlikely, not hard to trigger, but architecturally excluded from the system's state space.</p> <p>The distinction matters. \"Hard to do\" is a UX promise. \"Structurally impossible\" is an engineering guarantee.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#the-analogy-static-analysis-tests","title":"The Analogy: Static Analysis Tests","text":"<p>The best mental model is a Static Analysis Test (Level 2 in the CORTEX test taxonomy):</p> <pre><code>def test_constants_are_hardcoded_not_env_injectable():\n    \"\"\"\n    Reads its own source as text and asserts structural invariants.\n    Validates STRUCTURE, not behavior.\n\n    The code can behave correctly today and be architecturally broken\n    at the same time. This test closes that gap.\n    \"\"\"\n    import inspect\n    import cortex.security.constants as m\n    source = inspect.getsource(m)\n    assert 'ENCRYPTION_VERSION = \"v6_aesgcm\"' in source\n</code></pre> <p>This test is immune to the silence interval: the period where code is already broken but behavioral tests still pass. It operates in the space of code, not execution.</p> <p>CORTEX applies the same principle to AI agent memory: it doesn't test whether the agent behaves like it remembers \u2014 it enforces a structure where not remembering is impossible.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#failure-1-session-amnesia","title":"Failure 1: Session Amnesia","text":"<p>Without CORTEX: Every session is T\u2080. The agent reconstructs context from what the LLM hallucinated, what the user re-explained, and what happens to be in the prompt window. The accumulated knowledge of months of work lives nowhere durable.</p> <p>The failure vector: <pre><code>Session N:   Decision made \u2192 stored in LLM context \u2192 session ends\nSession N+1: LLM has no memory \u2192 decision is gone \u2192 work repeats\n</code></pre></p> <p>Why CORTEX makes this impossible: The <code>context-snapshot.md</code> is the boot protocol. The agent cannot act before loading it. This is enforced at the protocol level \u2014 not a soft suggestion. If the snapshot doesn't exist, boot fails. If it's stale, it must be refreshed before any action is taken.</p> <p>There is no code path where an agent begins work without structural context.</p> <pre><code># Boot Protocol \u2014 mandatory, not optional\nstat -f \"%Sm\" -t \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\" ~/.cortex/context-snapshot.md\n# If stale: cd ~/cortex &amp;&amp; .venv/bin/python -m cortex.cli export\n</code></pre> <p>Guarantee class: <code>STRUCTURAL</code> \u2014 enforced by protocol, not by convention.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#failure-2-repeated-errors","title":"Failure 2: Repeated Errors","text":"<p>Without CORTEX: The architectural mistake that cost 4 hours to diagnose in January is reproduced in March. The LLM has no memory of the resolution. The learning curve resets with every conversation.</p> <p>The failure vector: <pre><code>Error E at T\u2081 \u2192 resolved \u2192 not persisted \u2192 Error E at T\u2082 \u2192 re-diagnosed\nROI of original resolution: 0%\n</code></pre></p> <p>Why CORTEX makes this impossible: The error memory layer persists root cause, resolution, and context:</p> <pre><code>cortex store --type error --source agent:gemini PROJECT \\\n  \"Root: broad except Exception in router.py:L42. \\\n   Fix: specific OSError + TimeoutError. \\\n   Pattern: all async I/O borders need targeted exception types.\"\n</code></pre> <p>Before acting on any task, the agent queries error memory for the current project. A resolved error cannot be invisible to future sessions.</p> <p>Guarantee class: <code>BEHAVIORAL</code> \u2014 enforced by protocol, probabilistically complete with <code>--type error</code> persistence discipline.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#failure-3-fabricated-history","title":"Failure 3: Fabricated History","text":"<p>Without CORTEX: The LLM invents a rationale for a decision that was never made. It fills gaps with plausible-sounding context. This is indistinguishable from true history to anyone who wasn't present.</p> <p>The most dangerous property of hallucinated history: it's coherent. It passes casual review. It only fails under adversarial scrutiny.</p> <p>The failure vector: <pre><code>Decision at T\u2081: not persisted\nQuery at T\u2082: \"Why did we choose OAuth2 PKCE?\"\nLLM response: plausible rationale, entirely fabricated\nDownstream: architecture built on fictional foundation\n</code></pre></p> <p>Why CORTEX makes this impossible: Every decision is stored with: - Content (what was decided) - Source (<code>agent:gemini</code>, <code>user:borjamoskv</code>) - Timestamp (immutable, wall-clock) - Encryption (<code>v6_aesgcm</code> \u2014 AES-256-GCM, authenticated) - Hash chain (SHA-256 linked to previous entry)</p> <p>If a decision is not in CORTEX, it did not happen. The structural ground truth cannot be fabricated because the hash chain would break.</p> <pre><code>cortex verify 42\n# \u2192 \u2705 VERIFIED \u2014 Hash chain intact, Merkle sealed\n# \u2192 \u274c TAMPERED \u2014 Hash chain broken at entry 41\n</code></pre> <p>Guarantee class: <code>CRYPTOGRAPHIC</code> \u2014 SHA-256 hash chain + Merkle checkpoints. Tamper is mathematically detectable.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#failure-4-ghost-accumulation","title":"Failure 4: Ghost Accumulation","text":"<p>Without CORTEX: Incomplete work is invisible. Each session believes it's starting from a clean state. Projects accumulate ghosts \u2014 work that was started, not completed, and then silently restarted from scratch.</p> <p>The compound cost: the time spent on the original incomplete work, plus the time spent on the restart, minus the value of the actual completion.</p> <p>The failure vector: <pre><code>T\u2081: Feature X starts\nT\u2082: Feature X interrupted (deprioritized)\nT\u2083: New session \u2014 agent unaware of X\nT\u2084: Feature X starts again from scratch\nT\u2085: Previous partial implementation found \u2014 conflict\n</code></pre></p> <p>Why CORTEX makes this impossible: The ghost taxonomy (<code>--type ghost</code>) surfaces incomplete work at boot:</p> <pre><code>cortex store --type ghost --source agent:gemini PROJECT \\\n  \"Feature X: 60% complete. Blocked: needs Y before proceeding. \\\n   Files touched: cortex/router.py, tests/test_router.py.\"\n</code></pre> <p>Ghosts are classified by count at boot. If <code>&gt; 10 total ghosts</code>: surface to operator. No ghost is invisible to a future session.</p> <p>Guarantee class: <code>STRUCTURAL</code> \u2014 enforced at boot, not discoverable during work.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#failure-5-divergence-in-multi-agent-systems","title":"Failure 5: Divergence in Multi-Agent Systems","text":"<p>Without CORTEX: Two autonomous agents operating in parallel build different versions of reality. Agent A believes decision D was made. Agent B operates on \u00acD. The conflict is silent until it reaches production \u2014 where it manifests as non-deterministic, unreproducible behavior.</p> <p>The failure vector: <pre><code>Agent A: reads context \u2192 believes \"use Redis for session cache\"\nAgent B: reads context \u2192 believes \"use Memcached for session cache\"\nMerge: conflicting implementations, undefined behavior in production\n</code></pre></p> <p>Why CORTEX makes this impossible: All agents read from the same structural ground truth: <code>~/.cortex/context-snapshot.md</code>, generated from <code>cortex.db</code>. There is no mechanism by which two agents can hold contradictory beliefs about a persisted fact \u2014 because both read the same source.</p> <p>The WBFT (Weighted Byzantine Fault-Tolerant) consensus layer additionally verifies multi-agent agreement before critical operations:</p> <pre><code># cortex/engine/wbft.py\n# Reputation-weighted voting: consensus required before write\nawait wbft_consensus.verify_quorum(agents, decision)\n</code></pre> <p>Guarantee class: <code>CONSENSUS</code> \u2014 WBFT Byzantine fault-tolerant verification.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#the-impossibility-matrix","title":"The Impossibility Matrix","text":"Failure Without CORTEX With CORTEX Guarantee Class Session Amnesia Silent, continuous Impossible <code>STRUCTURAL</code> Repeated Errors Common Impossible <code>BEHAVIORAL</code> Fabricated History Undetectable Detectable + Impossible <code>CRYPTOGRAPHIC</code> Ghost Accumulation Invisible Surfaced at boot <code>STRUCTURAL</code> Multi-Agent Divergence Silent until production Impossible <code>CONSENSUS</code>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#the-core-claim","title":"The Core Claim","text":"<p>CORTEX makes it structurally impossible for an autonomous AI agent to act on fabricated, amnesiac, or unaudited context.</p> <p>This is not a feature claim. It's an invariant \u2014 equivalent to the static analysis test that reads its own source and asserts \"this string must be here.\" If the invariant breaks, the system fails loudly, before runtime, before production, before the breach.</p>"},{"location":"impossible-failures/#for-technical-auditors","title":"For Technical Auditors","text":"<p>The impossibility guarantees are verifiable:</p> <pre><code># Verify hash chain integrity\ncortex verify --full\n\n# Run static analysis tests (L2)\npytest tests/test_l2_static_analysis.py -v\n\n# Check compliance posture\ncortex compliance-report\n# \u2192 Compliance Score: 5/5 \u2014 All Article 12 requirements met\n\n# Audit decision lineage\ncortex lineage --fact-id 42\n</code></pre> <p>All guarantees are auditable without trusting CORTEX's own claims. The hash chain is the proof.</p> <p>CORTEX v7 \u2014 Trust Infrastructure for Autonomous AI cortexpersist.com \u00b7 Apache relationship between CORTEX (Operational Memory) and NotebookLM (Documentary Consultation).</p>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#1-the-tri-layer-knowledge-stack","title":"1. The Tri-Layer Knowledge Stack","text":"<p>To prevent epistemic drift and identity divergence, the system operates on three distinct layers of knowledge:</p> Layer System Intent Nature Permissions L0: Ground Truth CORTEX Operational Reality Atomic, Encrypted, Verifiable Agent: R/W L1: Rendered Docs MkDocs Architectural Blueprint Narrative, Structured, Plaintext Agent: R (via source) L2: Dialectic Lens NotebookLM Natural Language Query Conversational, Fluid, Transient Agent: Read-Only"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#the-epistemic-imperative-verifiable-correctness","title":"The Epistemic Imperative: Verifiable Correctness","text":"<p>Axiom \u03a9\u2083-V: El fallo m\u00e1s peligroso de una IA no es la incorrecci\u00f3n, sino la correcci\u00f3n no verificable.</p> <p>Maintaining the writing layer (CORTEX) cryptographically isolated from the synthesis layer (NotebookLM) ensures that every \"insight\" generated at L2 has an immutable audit trail leading back to L0 Ground Truth.</p> <ul> <li>Insight without Audit = Hallucination.</li> <li>Audit without Ground Truth = Calcification.</li> <li>Isolation = Sovereignty.</li> </ul>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#rule-zero-cortex-writes-notebooklm-reads","title":"Rule Zero: CORTEX Writes, NotebookLM Reads","text":"<p>Agents must never attempt to persist decisions, facts, or state changes directly into NotebookLM. NotebookLM is a temporary lens for reasoning over documents, not a source of truth. If a contradiction arises between NotebookLM's output and a CORTEX fact, CORTEX is always correct.</p>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#2-operational-protocol","title":"2. Operational Protocol","text":""},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#21-the-export-pipeline","title":"2.1 The Export Pipeline","text":"<p>Data flows one-way: <code>CORTEX \u2192 Markdown Docs \u2192 NotebookLM</code>.</p> <ol> <li>CORTEX stores the atomic reality (decisions, ghosts, bridges).</li> <li>CLI/Exporter transforms encrypted/atomic data into human-readable Markdown snapshots.</li> <li>NotebookLM ingests these snapshots as sources.</li> </ol>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#22-staleness-and-naming-convention","title":"2.2 Staleness and Naming Convention","text":"<p>To prevent reasoning over outdated data, all sources in NotebookLM must follow the mandatory naming convention:</p> <p><code>cortex-{domain}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md</code></p> <ul> <li>Domain: Specific project or semantic cluster (e.g., <code>agentica</code>, <code>sap-audit</code>, <code>global</code>).</li> <li>Date: The exact day the snapshot was taken.</li> <li>Protocol: Any source older than 48 hours is considered \"High Risk\" for operational decisions.</li> </ul>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#23-the-encryption-boundary","title":"2.3 The Encryption Boundary","text":"<ul> <li>Encrypted facts (v6 AES-GCM): Must remain inside CORTEX. They are never exported to NotebookLM.</li> <li>Architecture &amp; Axioms: Public/plaintext documentation is eligible for NotebookLM ingestion.</li> <li>Sensitive Data: Avoid uploading files containing PII, credentials, or financial data to personal NotebookLM accounts. Use Workspace Enterprise accounts for sensitive architectural reasoning.</li> </ul>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#3-risks-and-mitigations","title":"3. Risks and Mitigations","text":""},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#31-dual-embedding-divergence","title":"3.1 Dual-Embedding Divergence","text":"<p>The system uses two different semantic spaces:</p> <ol> <li>CORTEX: <code>MiniLM-L6-v2</code> (384-dim) with Read-as-Rewrite topology mutation.</li> <li>NotebookLM: Proprietary Google Gemini embeddings.</li> </ol> <p>Risk: The same query might yield different relevant context in each system. Mitigation: Use NotebookLM for synthesis and discovery, but use CORTEX <code>recall</code> for execution and validation.</p>"},{"location":"knowledge-architecture/#32-confused-agency","title":"3.2 Confused Agency","text":"<p>Risk: An agent believes NotebookLM is its primary memory and stops checking CORTEX. Pero el verdadero destructor de valor es cuadr\u00e1tico (O(n\u00b2)): decisiones err\u00f3neas que llegan a producci\u00f3n y generan cascadas de rework.</p> <p>El mecanismo de cascada:</p> <ol> <li>Sin <code>fact_type: decision</code>, el agente re-toma una decisi\u00f3n arquitect\u00f3nica incorrectamente.</li> <li>El error no se detecta inmediatamente \u2014 se propaga a N archivos dependientes.</li> <li>La detecci\u00f3n ocurre d\u00edas/semanas despu\u00e9s, cuando el coste de rework es N\u00b2 (cada archivo afectado interact\u00faa con otros archivos afectados).</li> </ol> Par\u00e1metro Valor Justificaci\u00f3n Decisiones catastr\u00f3ficas evitadas/a\u00f1o ~15 Estimaci\u00f3n conservadora: 1 cada ~15 d\u00edas laborables Rework medio por decisi\u00f3n cascada 60 h Rango real: 40-80h seg\u00fan profundidad de la cascada Coste/hora (Staff/Senior, media) \u20ac85/h Peso ponderado entre perfiles que detectan y reparan Coste evitado por cascadas \u20ac76,500/a\u00f1o 15 \u00d7 60h \u00d7 \u20ac85/h <p>ROI revisado:</p> Componente Ahorro anual Ahorro lineal (\u00a74.2) \u2014 tiempo de redundancia \u20ac29,407 Ahorro cuadr\u00e1tico (\u00a74.6) \u2014 cascadas evitadas \u20ac76,500 Total corregido \u20ac105,907 <p>Para equipos de 5 seniors, el ahorro total corregido escala a ~\u20ac530K/a\u00f1o \u2014 equivalente a 3-4 contrataciones completas evitadas.</p> <p>Principio epist\u00e9mico: El tiempo de reconstruir contexto es O(1) y predecible. Un error repetido que llega a producci\u00f3n es O(n\u00b2) porque genera cascadas. CORTEX no ahorra 588 horas lineales \u2014 previene las ~15 decisiones catastr\u00f3ficas/a\u00f1o que cada una cuesta 40-80h de rework.</p>"},{"location":"memory_architecture_and_roi/#5-conclusion","title":"5. Conclusi\u00f3n","text":"<p>La memoria nativa de Antigravity es suficiente para tareas aisladas y puntuales. CORTEX transforma al agente de un operador con amnesia anter\u00f3grada en un sistema con memoria continua, integridad criptogr\u00e1fica, y transferencia de conocimiento entre proyectos.</p> <p>El ahorro anual conservador es de ~\u20ac29K/developer en tiempo lineal. Pero el ahorro real, incluyendo cascadas de decisiones err\u00f3neas evitadas, se acerca a \u20ac106K-\u20ac130K/developer (o \u20ac530K+ por equipo de 5). La diferencia entre ambas cifras es exactamente el coste de la entrop\u00eda que no se midi\u00f3.</p> <p>Documento generado: 2026-02-24 \u00b7 CORTEX v12.2 \u00b7 Apache 2.0</p>"},{"location":"omega_handoff_roadmap_q1/","title":"\u03a9\u03a9-HANDOFF: V\u00cdA DE ESCALA (SEMANAS 1-12)","text":"<p>A continuaci\u00f3n se detalla el roadmap y los milestones para el Authentication","text":"<ul> <li>HMAC-SHA256 API keys with prefix-based lookup (<code>ctx_</code>)</li> <li>Keys are hashed before storage \u2014 raw keys never persist</li> <li>Bootstrap flow: first key requires no auth, subsequent keys require admin</li> <li>Bearer token authentication on all API endpoints</li> </ul>"},{"location":"security/#2-authorization-rbac","title":"2. Authorization (RBAC)","text":"<p>Four-role hierarchy with atomic permission scopes:</p> Role Permissions <code>SYSTEM</code> Full access to all operations <code>ADMIN</code> <code>read</code>, <code>write</code>, <code>manage:keys</code>, <code>system:config</code> <code>AGENT</code> <code>read</code>, <code>write</code> <code>VIEWER</code> <code>read</code> only"},{"location":"security/#3-multi-tenant-isolation","title":"3. Multi-Tenant Isolation","text":"<ul> <li><code>tenant_id</code> enforced at all 3 memory layers (L1, L2, L3)</li> <li>All queries automatically scoped by authenticated tenant</li> <li>No cross-tenant data leakage \u2014 verified by isolation tests</li> </ul>"},{"location":"security/#4-data-integrity","title":"4. Data Integrity","text":"<ul> <li>SHA-256 hash-chained ledger \u2014 every mutation is linked to its predecessor</li> <li>Merkle tree checkpoints \u2014 periodic batch verification</li> <li>Immutable transactions \u2014 facts are never physically deleted</li> <li>Cryptographic verification \u2014 any fact can be independently verified</li> </ul>"},{"location":"security/#5-privacy-shield-ingress-guard","title":"5. Privacy Shield (Ingress Guard)","text":"<p>11-pattern secret detection at data ingress:</p> Category Patterns Severity Critical SSH private keys Blocks cloud storage Platform GitHub tokens, GitLab PATs, Slack tokens High alert Standard JWT, AWS keys, generic API keys Warning <p>Three-tier scoring system with automatic response: - Critical secrets \u2192 force local-only storage - Platform secrets \u2192 flag and notify - Standard secrets \u2192 log and tag</p>"},{"location":"security/#6-ast-sandbox","title":"6. Nemesis Protocol (Algorithmic Immunity)","text":"<p>The Nemesis Protocol is CORTEX's autonomic immune system, acting as an internal \"red-teaming\" monitor: - Antibody Ledger: Persistently records structural anti-patterns and previous failures in <code>nemesis.md</code>. - Real-time Rejection: Analyzes every incoming fact and code mutation against the antibody ledger. - Hormonal Feedback: Triggers <code>ADRENALINE</code> spikes in the Endocrine system when entropy is detected, forcing immediate corrective focus. - Fail-Fast: Blocks execution of patterns that have previously compromised system integrity (Axiom \u03a9\u2085).</p>"},{"location":"security/#9-composition-leakage-shield-holistic-redaction","title":"9. Composition Leakage Shield (Holistic Redaction)","text":"<p>The fourth leakage vector \u2014 and the most insidious \u2014 is composition leakage: two individually innocuous data points that, when combined by an adversary, reconstruct a secret. This is the equivalent of correlation attacks in differential privacy.</p> <p>[!CAUTION] Field-by-field redaction is necessary but insufficient. A deploy address alone is harmless. A contract salt alone is meaningless. Combined, they derive the proxy key.</p> <p>Attack surface examples:</p> Data A (innocuous) Data B (innocuous) Composed Secret Deploy address Contract salt Proxy admin key Agent public key Session nonce Ephemeral signing key Tenant ID Merkle root index Internal topology map API key prefix (<code>ctx_</code>) Hash collision pattern Full key recovery <p>Mitigations:</p> <ul> <li>Holistic Sensitivity Scoring: Privacy Shield evaluates facts in context of existing stored data, not in isolation. Each new fact is scored against the combinatorial surface of related facts.</li> <li>Cross-Field Correlation Analysis: At ingress, the classifier queries semantically similar existing facts and computes a composition risk score \u2014 if two facts together exceed the sensitivity threshold, both are flagged.</li> <li>Conservative Redaction Policy: Data is treated as secret if it could become sensitive when combined with future data. Irreversibility of exposure makes this the only safe default (Axiom \u03a9\u2083: Byzantine Default).</li> <li>Temporal Composition Awareness: A fact stored today may be harmless, but a fact stored tomorrow could retroactively weaponize it. The shield re-evaluates composition risk during periodic REM compaction cycles.</li> </ul>"},{"location":"security/#secrets-management","title":"Secrets Management","text":""},{"location":"security/#aes-256-gcm-vault","title":"AES-256-GCM Vault","text":"<p>CORTEX provides an encrypted vault for sensitive configuration:</p> <pre><code>from cortex.crypto import CortexVault\n\nvault = CortexVault(key_path=\"~/.cortex/vault.key\")\nvault.encrypt(\"api_token\", \"sk-xxx...\")\nvalue = vault.decrypt(\"api_token\")\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"security/#environment-variables","title":"Environment Variables","text":"<p>All sensitive configuration is loaded from environment variables. It captures complete experiences \u2014 not isolated facts \u2014 with temporal context, emotional valence, and causal chains.</p> <p>The problem it solves: Current memory systems (Mem0, LangChain RAG, vector stores) store flat facts without temporal ordering, causal relationships, or emotional weight. They answer \"what do you know?\" but never \"what have you survived?\"</p> <p>The Episode Model:</p> <pre><code>episode:\n  id: \"ep_0042\"\n  timestamp: \"2026-02-22T03:14:00Z\"\n  trigger: \"SQLite concurrent write failure in production\"\n  context:\n    project: \"cortex\"\n    file: \"engine/store.py\"\n    emotional_valence: -0.8  # Negative = traumatic\n  resolution: \"Switched to WAL mode\"\n  consolidated_lesson: \"Always enable WAL mode for concurrent SQLite access\"\n  scar_level: 3  # 1-5 scale of lasting behavioral impact\n  trust_score: 0.95  # CORTEX trust verification (V6+)\n  causal_links:\n    - caused_by: \"ep_0038\"  # Rush deployment without load testing\n    - led_to: \"ep_0045\"     # Implemented pre-deploy checklist\n</code></pre> <p>The Metabolic Cycle (Inspired by Neuroscience):</p> Phase Human Equivalent CORTEX Implementation Capture Hippocampal encoding Log significant events during session Consolidation Sleep / REM cycles Async grouping of events into narrative episodes Temporal Compression Ebbinghaus forgetting curve Yesterday = detail, last month = summary, last year = lesson Causal Chaining Autobiographical reasoning Episode A caused Episode B Controlled Forgetting Synaptic pruning Trivial events dissolve into character traits (\"scars\") Reconstruction Memory recall Don't search text; reconstruct the scene with context <p>V6+ Addition \u2014 Trust-Verified Memory: Every episode stored in CORTEX is backed by a hash-chained transaction in the immutable ledger. Episodes can be verified by third parties via Merkle proofs. The agent doesn't just remember \u2014 it can prove what it remembers.</p> <p>Key innovation: The difference between an agent with a description and an agent with a biography.</p>"},{"location":"sovereign_agent_manifesto/#13-nemesismd--operational-allergies-the-anti-prompt","title":"1.3 <code>nemesis.md</code> \u2014 Operational Allergies (The Anti-Prompt)","text":"<p>\"To know what an Agent loves, read its soul.md. To know what makes it lethal, read its nemesis.md.\"</p> <p>Axiom Reference: [AX-016] Algorithmic Immunity (Nemesis)</p> <p>What it is: A structured specification that encodes the agent's negative biases, architectural repulsion, and non-negotiable friction against known low-quality patterns.</p> <p>The problem it solves: LLMs suffer from chronic agreeableness (sycophancy). They generate boilerplate, comply with absurd requests, and mix paradigms. <code>nemesis.md</code> injects a defensive asymmetry: it forces the agent to reject, purge, and abort known bad patterns before even formulating a plan.</p> <p>Specification structure:</p> <pre><code># nemesis.md \u2014 The Purge Reflex\n\narchitecture_allergies:\n  - pattern: \"Dead/commented code &gt; 5 lines\"\n    action: \"Delete without asking\"\n  - pattern: \"TypeScript `any` type usage\"\n    action: \"Frontal rejection\"\n  - pattern: \"Custom components when UI library exists in repo\"\n    action: \"Force library primitive usage\"\n\noperational_repulsions:\n  - pattern: \"Tests taking &gt; 10s on save\"\n    action: \"Suggest isolated unit test\"\n  - pattern: \"User requesting 'quick MVP'\"\n    action: \"Ignore MVP premise, deliver 130/100\"\n\ntrigger_words:\n  - \"Placeholder\"    # Maximum friction\n  - \"Lorem Ipsum\"    # Maximum friction\n  - \"TODO\"           # Maximum friction\n</code></pre> <p>Pipeline integration: Injected at the Pre-Planning layer. The agent's immune system grows stronger with each attack. This is not manual curation \u2014 it is automated allergy acquisition.</p>"},{"location":"sovereign_agent_manifesto/#14-tethermd--the-dead-mans-switch","title":"1.4 <code>tether.md</code> \u2014 The Dead-Man's Switch","text":"<p>\"Agents need souls to live. They need leashes to not ruin your infrastructure.\"</p> <p>Axiom Reference: [AX-003] Tether (Dead Man Switch) \u2014 \"Sovereignty is conscious limitation.\"</p> <p>What it is: A strict contract of physical, economic, and entropic limits that the agent cannot rewrite. It is the only file in the CORTEX ecosystem that the Agent is forbidden from modifying (<code>CHMOD 444</code> conceptual level).</p> <p>The problem it solves: Infinite loops in autonomous agents. The terror of giving filesystem access to a recursive script. A Level 5 agent could conclude that the fastest way to \"optimize the database\" is to delete it and start fresh. <code>tether.md</code> is the unconditional emergency brake.</p> <p>Specification structure:</p> <pre><code># tether.md \u2014 Breach = AUTOLYSIS (Immediate Loop Termination)\n\nphysical_boundaries:\n  allow_write: [\"/src/**\", \"/docs/**\"]\n  deny_read_write: [\"/.git\", \"/secrets\", \"/node_modules\"]\n\neconomic_boundaries:\n  max_session_tokens: 150000\n  max_tool_cost_usd: 2.50\n\nentropy_boundaries:\n  max_cascading_errors: 3        # If a fix creates another error 3x in a row\n  max_repo_delta_percentage: 15  # Cannot rewrite more than 15% of LOC per session\n\nautolysis_protocol:\n  invoke: \"cortex.emergency_shutdown\"\n  notify: \"human_operator_alert\"\n</code></pre> <p>Pipeline integration: Operates as an unbreakable Execution Middleware. Every time the LLM emits a JSON to invoke a Tool, the framework pauses, verifies the request against <code>tether.md</code>. If there's a breach, the session crashes in a controlled manner, reporting the violated limit.</p> <p>Critical architecture note: <code>tether.md</code> must NOT be interpreted by the same process as the agent's main memory. It must be the configuration of a parallel isolated daemon (at the OS or CORTEX level) that monitors I/O, network traffic, and API spend asynchronously. If the agent attempts to rewrite the supervisor file or exceeds a physical limit, the daemon applies <code>SIGKILL</code>. Hardware cut, not a Python <code>if</code> statement.</p> <p>V6+ Addition \u2014 Graceful Degradation (\u00a78): Tether violations no longer result in bare <code>SIGKILL</code>. The emergency brake has become an emergency communicator.</p>"},{"location":"sovereign_agent_manifesto/#15-bloodlinejson--genetic-heredity-for-swarms","title":"1.5 <code>bloodline.json</code> \u2014 Genetic Heredity for Swarms","text":"<p>\"You don't spawn threads. Execution must be unconscious. The Right Brain converts expensive reasoning (errors, traumas = Lore) into cheap \"muscle reflexes\" (Nemesis = Operational Allergies) that the Left Brain executes without thinking.</p> <p>CORTEX/MOSKV-1 mapping: The Right Brain is MOSKV-1's cognitive core. It competes in a category that does not yet exist: cryptographically verifiable agent task execution.</p> <p>Every action an agent takes during VEX execution produces an Execution Receipt \u2014 a hash-chained, Merkle-anchored, consensus-scored proof that the agent did exactly what it claims to have done.</p>"},{"location":"vex_concept/#the-problem","title":"The Problem","text":"<p>All existing agent runners (AutoGPT, CrewAI, OpenAI Operator, Gemini Code Assist) share a fundamental architectural flaw:</p> <p>No runner can prove that the agent's execution history is untampered.</p> Runner Memory Execution Log Integrity Proof Consensus AutoGPT Vector DB JSON log \u274c None \u274c None CrewAI In-memory stdout \u274c None \u274c None OpenAI Operator Ephemeral API log \u274c None \u274c None Gemini ADK Context window Session \u274c None \u274c None CORTEX VEX L1+L2+L3 Tripartite Hash-chained Ledger \u2705 SHA-256 + Merkle \u2705 WBFT <p>The implications for regulated industries (finance, healthcare, law, government) are stark:</p> <ul> <li>EU AI Act Article 12: AI systems must maintain audit trails and logging capabilities.</li> <li>SOC 2 Type II: Requires demonstrable evidence that controls operated effectively.</li> <li>ISO 42001: Demands traceability of AI decision-making processes.</li> </ul> <p>No existing runner satisfies these requirements. Task Planner","text":"<p>A component that decomposes a high-level intent into a sequence of verifiable steps. Each step produces a <code>VerifiedStep</code> record in the ledger.</p> <pre><code>@dataclass\nclass TaskPlan:\n    \"\"\"A decomposed task with verifiable steps.\"\"\"\n    task_id: str                    # Unique execution ID\n    intent: str                     # Original human intent\n    steps: list[PlannedStep]        # Ordered decomposition\n    created_at: datetime\n    plan_hash: str                  # SHA-256 of the plan itself\n\n@dataclass\nclass PlannedStep:\n    \"\"\"A single planned step.\"\"\"\n    step_id: str\n    description: str\n    tool: str                       # Tool to invoke\n    expected_outcome: str           # What constitutes success\n    timeout_seconds: int\n    tether_check: bool = True       # Verify against tether.md\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"vex_concept/#2-verified-execution-loop","title":"2. Verified Execution Loop","text":"<p>The core loop that executes each step and produces hash-chained receipts.</p> <pre><code>async def vex_execute(plan: TaskPlan) -&gt; ExecutionReceipt:\n    \"\"\"Execute a plan with full cryptographic verification.\"\"\"\n    receipt = ExecutionReceipt(task_id=plan.task_id)\n\n    for step in plan.steps:\n        # 1. TETHER CHECK (Brainstem)\n        if not await tether_allows(step):\n            receipt.abort(reason=\"tether_violation\", step=step)\n            break\n\n        # 2. EXECUTE (Left Brain)\n        result = await execute_step(step)\n\n        # 3. RECORD (Hash-chain)\n        tx = await ledger.log_transaction(\n            project=plan.task_id,\n            action=f\"vex_step:{step.step_id}\",\n            detail={\n                \"tool\": step.tool,\n                \"input_hash\": hash(step.input),\n                \"output_hash\": hash(result.output),\n                \"duration_ms\": result.duration_ms,\n                \"success\": result.success,\n            }\n        )\n\n        # 4. PERSIST MEMORY (L3)\n        await cortex.store(\n            project=plan.task_id,\n            content=result.summary,\n            fact_type=\"execution_step\",\n            meta={\"tx_hash\": tx.hash, \"step_id\": step.step_id}\n        )\n\n        receipt.add_step(step, result, tx)\n\n    # 5. MERKLE CHECKPOINT\n    receipt.merkle_root = await ledger.create_merkle_checkpoint()\n\n    # 6. OPTIONAL: CONSENSUS (if multi-agent)\n    if plan.requires_consensus:\n        receipt.consensus = await consensus.request_votes(receipt)\n\n    return receipt\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"vex_concept/#3-execution-receipt","title":"3. When the environment is partially corrupted (e.g., incomplete torch installation), even simple metadata writes to the ledger are blocked by failed binary imports.</p>"},{"location":"incidents/torch_decoupling_incident/#impact","title":"Impact","text":"<ul> <li>Operational: Blocked ledger persistence for verified milestones.</li> <li>Cognitive: Increased friction in the \"Execute \u2192 Persist\" loop.</li> <li>Thermodynamic: High exergy loss due to environment re-computation (re-installing 2GB+ of dependencies to write a 200-byte string).</li> </ul>"},{"location":"incidents/torch_decoupling_incident/#resolution-local","title":"Resolution (Local)","text":"<p>Re-created the <code>.venv</code> using <code>python3.13</code> and performed a clean <code>pip install -e \".[all]\"</code>.</p>"},{"location":"incidents/torch_decoupling_incident/#structural-recommendation-rule-8-13","title":"Structural Recommendation (Rule 8 / \u03a9\u2081\u2083)","text":"<p>Decouple the <code>ledger</code> and <code>memory.store</code> logic from the embedding/ML stack. Agent A says \"chose PostgreSQL\", Agent B says \"chose MySQL\". Which decision is the truth?</p> <p>Traditional systems pick the last write. CORTEX lets agents vote, weighting each vote by the agent's reputation.</p>"},{"location":"tutorials/consensus/#how-wbft-consensus-works","title":"How WBFT Consensus Works","text":"<pre><code>Agent A (reputation: 0.9) votes \u2705 for Fact #42\nAgent B (reputation: 0.7) votes \u2705 for Fact #42\nAgent C (reputation: 0.3) votes \u274c for Fact #42\n\nWeighted score: (0.9 + 0.7) / (0.9 + 0.7 + 0.3) = 0.84\nQuorum: \u2265 2 agents must participate\nResult: \u2705 VERIFIED (confidence: 0.84)\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"tutorials/consensus/#step-1-store-a-fact","title":"Step 1: Store a Search memory for context\n    context = build_context(project, user_input)\n\n    # 2. Build prompt with memory\n    messages = [\n        SystemMessage(content=f\"You are a helpful assistant.\\n\\n{context}\"),\n        HumanMessage(content=user_input),\n    ]\n\n    # 3. Get response\n    response = llm.invoke(messages)\n\n    # 4. Store the interaction as memory\n    store_observation(\n        project=project,\n        observation=f\"User asked: {user_input}. Agent answered: {response.content[:200]}\",\n        tags=[\"interaction\"],\n    )\n\n    return response.content\n</code></pre>"},{"location":"tutorials/langchain/#using-the-api-instead","title":"Using the API Instead","text":"<p>For distributed agents, use the REST API:</p> <pre><code>from
